programme.desc: Mike, Robert, and the Black Dog Salvage crew salvage parts off a former navy ship, the USS Zuni/ USCG Tamaroa. Key finds include an officer's table, portholes, and wooden ship frames. At the warehouse, Mike builds a table from an airplane wing.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Restored: 1947 Postmodern Ranch
programme.desc: Brett restores a 1947 postmodern ranch back to its post-World War II style for a military veteran and his wife. However, he has to make some tough decisions when the homeowners want to get rid of the original kitchen tile.

programme.title: Restored: 1905 Queen Anne Victorian Restoration
programme.desc: One of Brett's old high school friends asks him to help restore her 1905 Queen Anne Victorian bungalow. She has many unfinished renovation projects but wants to finish them. She also wants to convert a bedroom into the office it was designed to be.

programme.title: Restored: The Yellow Victorian Farmhouse Facelift
programme.desc: Brett restores a Victorian farmhouse with an odd floor plan. Major foundation issues complicate the project, but the happy homeowners end up with a beautiful and functional house they could never have imagined.

programme.title: Restored: 1901 Neoclassical Victorian
programme.desc: Brett helps an engaged couple restore their Victorian home, including a new kitchen and bathroom before their big wedding day. Brett begins work on the kitchen, removing additions and wallpaper.

programme.title: Restored: 1900 Victorian Farmhouse
programme.desc: A couple seeks Brett's help restoring their big 1900 Victorian Farmhouse in time for their wedding. They're hoping he can design a rustic kitchen, update the laundry area and add light to the interior rooms closed off by past modifications.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Sunrise Day Camp Clubhouse
programme.desc: Pete constructs an amazing clubhouse for Sunrise Day Camp: a summer camp for kids battling cancer. This open-air treehouse grants campers full interaction with nature by erasing the line between indoors and out.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Home Tree Home
programme.desc: A treehouse escape grows into a full-time live-in treehouse when Pete lays eyes on the gargantuan Sitka spruce of his dreams for a hexagonal build on the coast of Oregon.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Nature's Super HQ
programme.desc: Pete and NTS give superhero efforts to build a treehouse for the For-Mar Nature Preserve just outside Flint, MI. The 5-platform 'nature headquarters' will serve as a positive addition to a community still reeling from the water crisis that began in 2014.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Glasshouse Treehouse
programme.desc: Pete treks to New York's Catskill Mountains to build an NTS first - a glasshouse in the sky. Piecing materials together like a puzzle, Pete uses his clients' collection of 50 vintage windows to construct an elegant treehouse encased by walls of glass.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Bayou Barbecue and Saucy Skillets
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in Las Vegas, Nev., with a skillet full of pork belly chili and a mega-pancake that's the ultimate collision of apple pie and brunch.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Wild Smoke and Fiery Burgers
programme.desc: In Palm Springs, Calif., Michael Symon finds a spicy burger that ignites a four-alarm fire, and a chorizo-pineapple burger sings a perfect harmony of sweet and heat.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Sweet and Smokey
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in his hometown of Cleveland, where a place he went as a kid combines several classic sweets to make a one-of-a-kind dessert burger.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Hot and Spicy
programme.desc: Michael Symon digs into a spicy burger that uses four different types of peppers to fire up even the most adventurous eaters in Hell's Kitchen, New York City, before heading to Nashville, where he finds Southern comforts infused with Latin flavors.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Campfire Creations
programme.desc: Noah follows smoke signals to the fairground. He chows down on midway food inspired by cooking over an open campfire.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Nacho, Nacho Man
programme.desc: Noah Cappe tries some of the wackiest and wildest nacho combinations on the midway.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Bacon 2: The Sequel
programme.desc: Bacon is the star of its own blockbuster sequel and host Noah Cappe is at the front of line to savor even more bacon-tastic fairground favs.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Garlic's Angels
programme.desc: Noah Cappe packs his breath mints and heads to the South Florida Garlic Festival in Delray Beach, Fla., where the stinking rose makes an appearance in fair foods like the claw cake burger and mojo pork pockets.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Dates on a Plane
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives in the California desert for a date with deliciousness at the Riverside County Fair and National Date Festival. He tries spicy treats like the fire fingers kartwheel and the sonoran fire dog.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Mad Snacks: Berry Road
programme.desc: Noah Cappe travels back in time at the Texas Renaissance Festival, where the medieval-inspired meals range from the Sir Francis fry dog to the crown jewel. Then it's over to Plant City, Fla., where Noah chows down on some berry delicious carnival treats.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Guilty Pleasures
programme.desc: Roger Mooking showcases fifteen of the most delightfully decadent dishes from around America. From bacon brownies to chicken fried lobster, they're the type of savory pleasures and sweet sensations that make everyone long for 'cheat day'.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Feasting at the Fair
programme.desc: We're traveling to the fairest of state fairs in the nation. To taste the best of the best, we chow down in the Land of Enchantment, sample old favorites in New England and see what treats are whippin' across people's lips in Oklahoma.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Happy Appy Hour
programme.desc: Forget the free pretzels and peanuts, these joints have upped the Happy Hour ante with outrageous, tummy-tantalizing appetizers, from a Bloody Mary topped with spaghetti and meatballs to lamb chop fondue and chicken wing confit.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Halifax
programme.desc: Chef Jordan Andino heads up Canada's east coast to check out the nightlife in Halifax, Nova Scotia. He starts at The Watch That Ends the Night for one of the best grilled cheese sandwiches he's ever had.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Global Grub
programme.desc: Roger Mooking takes 15 gastronomical trips for the most exotic, flavourful and spicy foods all without leaving the U.S. The featured chefs create classic international meals from Ethiopia, Thailand, Mexico and more in their own American kitchens.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Smoked
programme.desc: Roger Mooking criss-crosses America to count down 15 of the very best steaks, wings, Reubens and more- all made even better with glorious smoke. From salt-cured country ham to smoked fish chowder, bacon bourbon rib-eye steak to smoky s'mores pancakes.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Mud and Steel in New Mexico
programme.desc: Roger heads to the heart of New Mexico where cooking with fire is taken to new culinary heights with mud and steel. Roger visits Comida de Campos in Embudo, a farm and cooking school where delicious feasts are cooked in cone-shaped clay ovens.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Carnivore's Cookout in California
programme.desc: Roger is in central California for two meat-filled cookouts. This region is home to many vineyards, but Paso de Record Vineyard is what catches Roger's interest.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: West Coast Roasts
programme.desc: Visiting the Passmore Ranch in Sloughhouse, Roger must try to catch a fish by hand. Also, at the Santa Maria Elks Lodge, he will assist in building a fire pit for cooking up some beef.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Wilderness Cooking
programme.desc: Roger visits the Jack Mountain Bushcraft School in Masardis, Maine where the owner and wilderness guide Tim Smith teaches him how to create a rustic kitchen outdoors.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Fireplace Feasts
programme.desc: Roger loves cooking in a fireplace. He visits two places in New England that host fireplace feasts.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Playing With Fire in New England
programme.desc: Roger meets two people in New England who love to play with fire. In Plymouth, Massachusetts, Paula Marcoux has a backyard full of cooking contraptions inspired by her travels.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Deliciously Doughy

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Return of the Mac

programme.desc: Mira's friends help solve the case when she loses a game that's been passed down through her family. When the archery arrows go missing during the Jalpur Games, Mira sets out to find them.

programme.desc: Candace thinks she finally has a strange device to show Mom and bust Phineas and Ferb when Agent P knocks Dr. Doofenshmirtz's latest '-inator' into the boys' front yard. Phineas and Ferb then reconstruct the device to find out what it does.

programme.title: Amphibia: Anne Theft Auto / Breakout Star
programme.desc: A fearless teen is magically transported to a rural marshland full of frog people.

programme.title: Amphibia: Truck Stop Polly/ A Caravan Named Desire
programme.desc: Feeling unappreciated by her family, Polly hatches a plan to make them pay attention to her. Hop Pop signs the family up to join a traveling theater troupe, hoping to realize his dream of being an actor.

programme.title: The Owl House: I Was a Teenage Abomination

programme.title: The Owl House: Covention

programme.title: The Owl House: The Intruder

programme.title: The Owl House: Lost in Language
programme.desc: Luz's pranks at the library lead to unintended consequences.

programme.title: The Owl House: Adventures in the Elements

programme.title: The Owl House: The First Day

programme.title: The Owl House: Sense and Insensitivity
programme.desc: When King becomes a bestselling author, he learns a valuable lesson in reading the fine print.

programme.title: The Owl House: Escape of the Palisman

programme.title: The Owl House: Enchanting Grom Fright

programme.title: The Owl House: Witches Before Wizards
programme.desc: Luz receives a map for a mystical quest from an ancient wizard.

programme.title: The Owl House: Really Small Problems
programme.desc: King confides in a carnival fortune teller who makes his dream come true, but it comes at a cost.

programme.title: Funny You Should Ask

programme.title: Black-ish: Hope

programme.title: Nancy Drew: The Girl in the Locket

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Just Once With Aunt Sophie

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Arguments for the Quickie
programme.desc: When Charlie finds out that Mia is in town with her dance troupe, he opts not to see her and tries to convince Alan that he's over her. Charlie gets another surprise when Mia explains that she wants something personal from him, his sperm.

programme.title: The Goldbergs: Dinner With the Goldbergs

programme.title: Mom: Nudes and a Six-Day Cleanse

programme.title: Last Man Standing: Stud Muffin
programme.desc: The Baxters' neighbour Chuck Larabee stops by with his german shepherd, Lady, to let them know that their tiny dog, Muffin, has impregnated his dog. Chuck is very selective about who Lady breeds with because purebred german shepherd puppies are in demand.
